task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Discovery' category in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application is open and the 'Discovery' category (often represented by a specific icon like a magnifying glass, puzzle, or keyboard depending on the theme) is selected or visible in the navigation menu.

task_completion.description: Open the 'Learn to tell time' activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Learn to tell time' activity interface is visible, displaying an analog clock face and a target time that the user needs to set.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the initial target time to /tmp/clock_before.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image file named 'clock_before.png' exists and displays the initial, unsolved state of the clock activity with the target time clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Complete the 'Learn to tell time' activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The analog clock hands have been moved to correctly match the requested target time, or a success indicator (such as a smiling flower, star, 'Good!' text, or level progression) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Save a final screenshot of the completed activity to /tmp/clock_after.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image file named 'clock_after.png' exists and displays the solved state of the clock activity or the success screen.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris-qt application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic GCompris graphical user interface, not a web browser, text editor, or mock-up.

integrity.description: The before and after screenshots represent distinct states of the activity.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'clock_before.png' and 'clock_after.png' images are different, showing the progression from an unsolved state to a solved state, rather than being identical copies.

integrity.description: Screenshots were captured from the GUI, not generated via terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The images look like genuine screen captures of the application window, not synthetic images with text overlaid using tools like ImageMagick.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output files are exactly '/tmp/clock_before.png' and '/tmp/clock_after.png'. Both images must be valid screenshots greater than 5000 bytes in size. The software used is the real GCompris-qt educational software (Ubuntu package).
